description: Synthetic corpus: vague/generic BQ column names containing PII content. Validates that classification works when column_name provides zero signal.
match_ratio_note: When column_name is uninformative, match_ratio reflects pure content-signal prevalence. A structured_single column with all-email values should have match_ratio near 1.0. A free_text_heterogeneous column with mixed PII may have lower match_ratio per entity type since each value may contain different entity types.

columns.expected_entity_type_note: sk-proj-* keys get classified as OPAQUE_SECRET via entropy handler when regex API_KEY pattern does not match the specific prefix

SHA-256 hashes are high-entropy opaque tokens; the entropy handler classifies them as OPAQUE_SECRET. This is arguably correct behavior - hashes could be password hashes or other sensitive derived data.
